paragraphs.body: Metaphorical speech has been a source of connection and identity within my family, as my parents always utilized it to inspire me. Have you ever heard a phrase so often that it becomes a part of you, echoing in your mind like your best friend offering advice? For me those words came from my ultimate inspiration, my parents. In my household, our words were not the only source of comfort, as my parents also showed their love through actions. This could be as simply as my mom making my favorite meal because I had a bad day, or my dad washing my car because I had a busy week and could not get around to it. However, the words that they have always told me are always ringing in the back of my mind reminding me of the love and wisdom I continue to carry forward with me forever.

annotations.comment: I'd still like to see a more intentional intro/conclusion form. I see the question you're asking, though :)

paragraphs.body: Initially, I was so confused as to what my parents’ sayings meant. I often thought that they were quite funny. A few of consisted of “people who live in glass houses should not throw stones” or “you catch more flies with honey then you do vinegar.” But the one that has stuck with me the most is, “You can lead a horse to water, but you cannot make them drink it.” This quote means that you can show someone an amazing opportunity, yet it does not mean they will take it. It is impossible to force someone to take an opportunity. Growing up, I thought that this quote was senseless, and did not give it much thought. However, once I got to high school, my mom repeated the quote, but this time it began to stick. Once I pondered the intention of my mom telling me this, I realized that it was all because my parents just wanted me to succeed; everything my parents did for me was all to help me. They wanted me to grow up to be successful, but most importantly, happy. They knew after they got a divorce this might be hard for me but they still continued to instill these ideas in me.

annotations.text: They knew after they got a divorce this might be hard for me but they still continued to instill these ideas in me.

paragraphs.body: Looking back on my childhood, in some ways, I despised my parents for getting a divorce because I felt like all this love they preached was fake. I was a small child at the time, so like every little kid with split parents, I tried to get my parents back together even though deep down I knew it would never work. Although difficult at first, I began to adjust quickly and maintain my close relationship with both of my parents. When parents are divorced, some kids may feel like they are lonely or are not loved anymore, but for me it is the total opposite. Although my home is split into two now, my parents have never stopped the “home” atmosphere in both houses. I constantly feel that they are both here for me and I always feel a sense of relief talking to anyone in my immediate family. Even though there are two houses, it is still one big home for me. Their encouragement and constant support have set me up to push myself out of my comfort zone and take risks. I was constantly reminded that they were there for me, and my mom’s quote would ring in my ears frequently as I kept taking risks within high school, reaching out to more and more people to form friendships. My family is where I feel safe and comforted, which is a key aspect of my success. They have created an atmosphere where I feel motivated to pursue my interests. However, I knew that they were always there for me, but just like my mom told me with the horse, her and my dad had directed me towards success, but it was up to me to truly achieve it.

annotations.text: Looking back on my childhood, in some ways, I despised my parents for getting a divorce because I felt like all this love they preached was fake. I was a small child at the time, so like every little kid with split parents, I tried to get my parents back together even though deep down I knew it would never work.

paragraphs.body: I finally understood what I meant on the first day of high school. At the time, I did not have many friends. However, I was invited to dinner by a group of girls, yet I passed on the opportunity because I was scared to go. Once I told my mom about the situation she repeated the quote. She told me that I had a great opportunity yet missed my chance. She said if you do not take risks in life, you will never succeed. It is only in failure that you truly succeed by learning from your mistakes.

My mom has done an indescribable number of things for me and wants nothing but the best for me. She is my role model and best friend, she gives me a sense of love every single day. Since being at college I’ve realized how much she actually loves me. The first two weeks here I was struggling really badly and struggled to make friends. I was very homesick and honestly overall hated it here. She stayed on the phone with me for hours every day just to make sure I was okay, and when she did not get to talk she would she me little care packages, again going back to love in my family is sometimes show more in deeds then in words. Whilst all of this was greatly appreciated, I remember that my mom had taught me that it was up to me to put myself out there and succeed on my own. While her support did help get me through the first few weeks, it was what she taught me that pushed me to finally put myself out there and make friends. Being strong has always been one of my mom’s strong suit due to the divorce, I just constantly reminded myself that she can lead me in the right direction, but it’s up to me, just like the horse.

annotations.text: I just constantly reminded myself that she can lead me in the right direction, but it’s up to me, just like the horse.

paragraphs.body: My mom and dad have both told me that I am a hard worker and I get what I want because I work for it. Every day this inspires me to make them proud through my successes. The feeling of telling them I studied for ten hours but then got a ninety on the exam brings me great joy and satisfaction. My parents have loved and supported me endlessly, setting me up for success as I now have a great sense of motivation and desire to make them proud.

To speak in love means My Mom teaching me to be strong, Though I struggled with high school they would never leave, Their love, a constant, steady and true, With each step as I spread my wings they are by my side
